Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We want to start sizing a Dedicated pool to support our inference pipeline for the genomics annotation service. High-level:
- Peak inference window: 09:00–18:00 PT weekdays
- Steady-state: ~120k tokens/minute (aggregate across endpoints)
- Micro-bursts: up to 1.2M tokens over 30s during batch ingests (rare, ~3x/day)
- Latency target (p95): <= 250ms for the primary model (13B transformer, decoder-only)

Date: Fri, 11 Apr 2025 09:12:00 -0700

Hi Ben,

We experienced a significant outage on Apr 10 between ~02:00–05:00 UTC that caused multiple failed requests and degraded latency across our production traffic. Our SRE team captured logs and a short summary (attached) — the incident impacted two key customer-facing endpoints and triggered automated failovers which still resulted in ~3 hours of elevated error rates.

We signed up for a pilot in March and are in the middle of a proof-of-value; this disruption materially affects our evaluation. Given the customer impact and the shortened evaluation window, we are requesting:

Subject: One-week clock drift between regions — demo scheduling options

Hi Anna / Pierre,

Thanks again for your time on the call earlier. Wed love to run a 45-minute product demo next week focused on latency/region routing and multi-tenant isolation for your EU teams. I wanted to flag one thing up front: the US spring-forward DST change happens a week before the EU clocks change this year, so the usual time conversions are off by an hour for one week. That often trips up global calendar coordination.

To avoid back-and-forth, here are three proposed slots that keep the shift in mind (times shown as local equivalents):

1) Tue Mar 11, 09:00 PT / 17:00 CET / 01:00 JST (next day) — earlier EU even...

Ava,

Ahead of next week’s beta enablement with the design partners, we did a quick risk review on the Quantization Profile Library rollout path.

Top risks we want to explicitly guard:
1) Quality regressions (offline evals pass but edge-case prompts regress).
2) Incompatibility (profile enabled on a route but traffic lands on unsupported GPU SKU / context length / model variant).
3) Control plane or registry load issues causing elevated 5xx (thundering herd on profile metadata fetch).

Proposal (what we need leadership approval on):

Subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines

Sa...
text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Desai <samir.desai@northpeakbank.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 18, 2025 at 4:12 PM
Subject: Re: Redwood Inference security review: retention & deletion timelines

Samir answering inline below.

1) Prompts/outputs retention: For Hosted API and Dedicated, we do not persistently store prompts and model outputs by default.
2) Request logs retention: We retain operational request logs (metadata only) for 14 days by default.
3) Debug payload logging: If you explicitly enable it for troubleshooting, retention is 7 days.
4) Audit logs: retained for 12 months.
5) Deletion requests: once validated, we complete deletion within 30 days for data stored in Redwood-managed systems.

Happy to jump on a quick call if helpful.

 Ava

--
Ava Chen
Enterpr...

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
Cc: NordBank Procurement <procurement@nordbank.example>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification

Hi Elin,

Confirming what we discussed: NordBank traffic will be served in EU (eu-west) under normal operations. We can support a *time-bounded exception* to allow failover serving in the US region if the EU region is degraded or during scheduled DR test windows.

Key point: we have payload logging disabled for your tenant, so we do not persist prompts/responses as logs. During failover, operational metrics (request ids, timings, error rates) may be processed in the failover region.

We’re drafting a short exception memo for your procurement file.

Thanks,
Camila

—

Hello Elena / EdgeBridge Ops,

Quick heads up: we plan to add EdgeBridge as a temporary hosting subprocessor for telemetry relay in the eu-west-1 region to support a short-term pilot for a European customer. The window would be approximately 6 weeks starting late June.

Required from your side for our DPA/compliance packet:
- Signed DPA addendum (we have a template; we can push via DocuSign)
- Updated dataflow diagram showing EU-only routing and egress points (PNG/PDF)
- Confirmation of encryption-in-transit and at-rest keys used for this job; KMS jurisdiction details
- Any SOC2 / security attestations you can s...

text:
From: Claire Newman <claire.newman@medbridge.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: MedBridge Security Team <security@medbridge.health>
Date: Tue, 7 May 2024 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Backup purge windows + DPA question

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our security lead asked for a clear plan for how Redwood will handle: (1) phased backup purges across regions, (2) how deletions are propagated to subprocessors, and (3) whether short-lived log retention exceptions (forensics) can be carved out of our DPA.

Can you share a proposed timeline and a short description of the operational handoff to subprocessors? We need this to feed the procurement SOW and legal review.

Thanks,
Claire Newman
Director of IT Security
MedBridge Health

(Attachment: medbridge-questionnaire-202405.zip)

Ben —

We're seeing a sustained set of slower completions across our traffic starting Monday evening. P95 looks...
text:
From: Thomas Li <thomas.li@novonow.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ops Team <ops@novonow.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Apr 2024 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Noticeable spike in completion delays this week

Ben —

We're seeing a sustained set of slower completions across our traffic starting Monday evening. P95 looks OK but a non-trivial fraction of requests are taking 6–12x the median and a subset are timing out. Error rate is creeping up (0.6% -> 1.8%) and throughput throttles aren't letting us recover. I've attached a sample set of request traces and a topology diagram.

We're on a committed tier (contract NW-5231) and this is impacting a checkout flow for several stores. Can you confirm receipt and advise next steps? We need an ETA on mitigation and whether you can temporarily prioritize our traffic.

Thanks,
Thomas Li
Head of Platform, Novonow
thomas.li@novonow.com

Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot...
text:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@helioscope.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sana Rao <sana@helioscope.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Mar 2024 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sizing question: Dedicated capacity for Q3 pilot

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the demo last week. We want to scope a small Dedicated deployment for a July pilot (NDA done). Quick background: our inference pattern is a mix of short chat completions and a reranking endpoint used by our search service. Typical request payloads are ~40–80 tokens; p95 tends to skew towards longer multi-turn flows.

Traffic profile (weekdays):
- Peak concurrent requests (observed): ~60 QPS on search rerank windows (bursty, 30s peaks).
- Average chat QPS outside peaks: 12–18.
- Average tokens per request: 55.
- 95th percentile tokens per request during peak windows: 140.

We care about p95 tail latency <= 300 ms for search rerank (single-turn) and < 700 ...

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Elaine Park <elaine.park@sanahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jan 2024 15:20:00 -0800
Subject: Discovery recap — align on demo window & security/procurement contacts

Hi Elaine —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap from our call and proposed next steps so we can keep momentum: 

- Pain: your team needs predictable inference latency for clinical summarization pipelines and strict data isolation for PHI.
- Key stakeholders to include in the next meeting: Security (to review isolation & SOC2 processes), Procurement (contracting & SLAs), Platform (networking & deployment preferences).

Next step: technical demo / deep dive where we show a short 20–25 minute walkthrough of a production flow, followed by a 25–30 minute Q&A focused on security and deployment...
